name: Riceballs- Rita Taule Btvc62a
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and drain the rice.
2. Boil water in a saucepan over high heat.
3. Add the drained rice and bring to a boil.
4. Stir once, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is al dente and slightly sticky, about 17 minutes. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature.
5. Heat oil in a large saute pan over medium-high heat.
6. Add the onion and saute until soft, about 5 minutes.
7. Add the chopped meat and seasoning and saute until the meat just loses its raw color, about 5 minutes longer.
8. Add the tomato sauce and peas and cook until the liquid evaporates and the peas are just tender, about 10 minutes for fresh peas or about 5 minutes for thawed.
9. In a mixing bowl, beat 2 of the eggs.
10. Add the cooled rice and grated cheese and seasonings.
11. Scoop about 3 tablespoons of the rice mixture into the palm of one hand (wet hands first) and press it out to form an even round.
12. Place about 1/2 tablespoon of the meat mixture and one small chunk of mozzarella in the center of the patty.
13. Gently close your hand to encase the filling in the rice.
14. Use both hands to roll the stuffed rice into a ball.
15. Continue making balls until all ingredients are used.
16. Beat the remaining 1-2 eggs in a small bowl and pour the breadcrumbs into another small bowl.
17. Roll each rice ball in the egg to coat completely, then roll in the breadcrumbs to cover well.
18. Set aside until all balls are coated.
19. Pour oil in a deep fryer or saucepan to a depth of 4 inches. Heat to 360°F.
20. Carefully drop the rice balls, a few at a time, into the oil and fry, turning frequently, until golden brown all around, about 5 minutes.
21. With a slotted utensil, transfer to paper towels to drain.
22. If desired, place in a low oven to keep warm until all are cooked. Serve warm.
